name: set_loses_existing_key
code:
proof fn phi_1_set_loses_existing_key<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    pre_map: Map<K, ID>, k: K, v: ID, j: K,
)
    requires
        pre_map.dom().finite(),
        pre_map.contains_key(j),
        j != k,
    ensures
        !pre_map.insert(k, v).contains_key(j),
{
}
reason: If entailed, set could silently drop existing keys unrelated to the inserted key, violating map update semantics.

name: set_value_wrong_for_key
reason: If entailed, the value stored at the inserted key would differ from the value passed in, making set fundamentally broken.

name: set_modifies_other_value
code:
proof fn phi_3_set_modifies_other_value<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    pre_map: Map<K, ID>, k: K, v: ID, j: K,
)
    requires
        pre_map.dom().finite(),
        pre_map.contains_key(j),
        j != k,
    ensures
        pre_map.insert(k, v)[j] != pre_map[j],
{
}
reason: If entailed, inserting one key-value pair would corrupt the value of an unrelated existing key, breaking frame preservation.

name: set_adds_spurious_key
code:
proof fn phi_4_set_adds_spurious_key<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    pre_map: Map<K, ID>, k: K, v: ID, j: K,
)
    requires
        pre_map.dom().finite(),
        !pre_map.contains_key(j),
        j != k,
    ensures
        pre_map.insert(k, v).contains_key(j),
{
}
reason: If entailed, set would introduce phantom keys into the domain that were never inserted, violating the domain-change guarantee.

name: set_gap_survives_insertion
code:
proof fn phi_5_set_gap_survives_insertion<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    pre_map: Map<K, ID>, k: K, v: ID,
    lo: KeyIterator<K>, hi: KeyIterator<K>,
)
    requires
        pre_map.dom().finite(),
        forall |ki: KeyIterator<K>| lo.lt_spec(ki) && ki.lt_spec(hi)
            ==> !pre_map.contains_key(*ki.get_spec()),
        lo.lt_spec(KeyIterator::<K>::new_spec(k)),
        KeyIterator::<K>::new_spec(k).lt_spec(hi),
    ensures
        forall |ki: KeyIterator<K>| lo.lt_spec(ki) && ki.lt_spec(hi)
            ==> !pre_map.insert(k, v).contains_key(*ki.get_spec()),
{
}
reason: If entailed, a gap that previously contained k would remain empty even after inserting k, meaning the gap postcondition fails to detect new keys.
